title: Principles of American government
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on the principles of American government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates the concept of “separation of powers” in the US Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress has authority over the federal budget, while the president appoints Supreme Court justices
Congress can pass a measure, while the president can veto it
Congress can pass a measure, while the Supreme Court can declare it unconstitutional
Mississippi can set educational policy for its students, while California can set education policy for its students

assessment_items.question_content:
A president issues an executive order banning newspapers from printing articles critical of the US government. The Supreme Court declares the executive order unconstitutional because it violates the First Amendment right to freedom of the press. 

**This is an example of which of the following principles of US government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Checks and balances
Federalism
Republicanism
Popular sovereignty

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates the concept of “separation of powers” in the US Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president commands the nation’s armed forces, while Congress has the power to ratify treaties
The president can create an executive order, while the Supreme Court can declare it unconstitutional
The president can nominate justices to the Supreme Court, while the Senate approves presidential nominations
States control elections and public education, while the federal government is responsible for making treaties and defending the country militarily

assessment_items.question_content:
Congress passes a law requiring social media companies to provide user data—including private messages, search histories, and location information—to federal law enforcement agencies upon request, without obtaining a warrant or user consent. The law is challenged in court by a civil liberties organization. The Supreme Court ultimately rules the law unconstitutional, holding that it violates the Fourth Amendment’s protection against unreasonable searches and seizures. The Court’s decision establishes that individuals have a reasonable expectation of privacy in their digital communications and that a warrant is required for government access to such data.

**Which constitutional principle is most clearly demonstrated in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Checks and balances among the branches of government
Separation of powers within the federal government
Popular sovereignty as the basis of constitutional authority
Legislative authority to regulate interstate commerce

assessment_items.question_content:
> "To what expedient, then, shall we finally resort, for maintaining in practice the necessary partition of power among the several departments, as laid down in the Constitution? The only answer that can be given is, that as all these exterior provisions are found to be inadequate, the defect must be supplied, by so contriving the interior structure of the government as that its several constituent parts may, by their mutual relations, be the means of keeping each other in their proper places."

> -Publius (James Madison), *Federalist No. 51*, 1788

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Madison’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The structure of the government itself should limit its powers
Most methods for controlling government are inadequate
The current structure of government is defective
Government should keep people in their proper places

assessment_items.question_content:
>" To what expedient, then, shall we finally resort, for maintaining in practice the necessary partition of power among the several departments, as laid down in the Constitution? The only answer that can be given is, that as all these exterior provisions are found to be inadequate, the defect must be supplied, by so contriving the interior structure of the government as that its several constituent parts may, by their mutual relations, be the means of keeping each other in their proper places."

> -Publius (James Madison), *Federalist No. 51*, 1788

**Which of the following principles of US government is most consistent with the author’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Checks and balances
Participatory democracy
Natural rights
Social contract

assessment_items.question_content:
>"In a single republic, all the power surrendered by the people is submitted to the administration of a single government; and the usurpations are guarded against by a division of the government into distinct and separate departments. In the compound republic of America, the power surrendered by the people is first divided between two distinct governments, and then the portion allotted to each subdivided among distinct and separate departments. Hence a double security arises to the rights of the people. The different governments will control each other, at the same time that each will be controlled by itself."

> -Publius (James Madison), *Federalist No. 51*, 1788

**Which of the following principles of US government is most consistent with the author’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Separation of powers
Republicanism
Consent of the governed
Natural rights

assessment_items.question_content:
> “To what expedient, then, shall we finally resort, for maintaining in practice the necessary partition of power among the several departments...the defect must be supplied, by so contriving the interior structure of the government as that its several constituent parts may...be the means of keeping each other in their proper places.”

>-Publius (James Madison), *Federalist No. 51* 

**According to Madison, how does the structure of the U.S. government under the Constitution help prevent the abuse of power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It divides power across independent branches that can limit each other.
It gives the federal government authority over state governments.
It ensures that only elected officials can serve in any branch of government.
It lets the majority bypass checks and concentrate power by electing leaders who can weaken safeguards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a consequence of the principle of separation of powers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There are multiple access points for stakeholders and institutions to influence public policy
The presidency has become more powerful than the other branches of government
There is an easy and efficient process for the government to pass and execute laws
State governments have become more powerful than the federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
Members of the National Organization for Women (NOW) protest a congressional bill that eliminates protections for pregnant workers. After the bill passes, they form a letter-writing campaign hoping to influence the president to veto the bill. 

**This is an example of which of the following principles of American government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Multiple access points for stakeholders to influence policymaking
Government-mandated healthcare coverage
The central role of lobbying organizations in passing legislation
The shift in political power from the legislative branch to the executive branch

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2015, the Supreme Court ruled that the Fourteenth Amendment guarantees people of the same sex the right to marry in the same manner as opposite-sex couples. Following this ruling, the National Organization for Marriage appealed to members of Congress encouraging them to pass an amendment to the Constitution defining marriage as a union between a man and a woman.

**This is an example of which of the following principles of American government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Multiple access points for stakeholders to influence policymaking
Commitment to provide a republican form of government in every state
Executive veto of legislation
Power of the purse in Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
A coalition of environmental groups opposes a new law that reduces regulations on carbon emissions. After the bill passes in Congress, the groups organize a petition urging the president to veto the bill. When the president signs the bill into law, the groups file a lawsuit, arguing that the law violates existing environmental protections.

**Which constitutional principle is most clearly illustrated by this scenario?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Interest groups using multiple access points in government
Citizens shaping law through direct democratic votes
State governments overruling national environmental policy
Congress interpreting laws passed by other branches

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following may Congress do to limit the power of the executive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Impeach the president
Declare an executive order unconstitutional
Veto a bill in the Senate
Nominate a Supreme Court justice

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the most immediate consequence of impeaching a president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president is tried in the Senate
The president is removed from office
The president is tried by the Supreme Court
The president is sent into exile

assessment_items.question_content:
**Removal of a public official for crimes or abuse of power is an example of which of the following principles of American government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Checks and balances
Republicanism
Federalism
Policymaking complexity

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which action best illustrates Congress using its constitutional authority to formally respond to serious misconduct by a federal official?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Impeaching and removing the official through a formal process
Investigating the official through a joint congressional committee
Passing a resolution condemning the official’s actions
Releasing a public statement criticizing the official’s actions
